skill.name: git
skill.description: Inspect Git repository state with read-only commands for status, diffs, branches, history, remotes, and tracked files.
skill.summary: Run focused read-only Git inspections for repository review and change analysis.
skill.author: ETeissonniere

security_audit.summary: Static external command findings are true positives because the skill instructs agents to run Git CLI commands. The commands are read-only and fit the stated purpose, but they can reveal repository metadata, diffs, commit history, and remote URLs. The weak cryptography findings at SKILL.md lines 3 and 10 are false positives from prose and show no cryptographic operation.

medium_findings.title: Read-only Git Command Execution
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: SKILL.md lists many Git CLI commands for repository inspection. This is legitimate for a Git skill, but command execution can expose local repository state, diffs, branch names, commit history, tracked files, and remote URLs to the agent output.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file clearly contains Git commands intended for shell execution. The commands are read-only and match the skill purpose, so the risk is operational exposure rather than malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The static scanner flagged SKILL.md lines 3 and 10 as weak cryptography, but those lines are prose describing basic Git operations. No evidence found of cryptographic algorithms, hashing functions, encryption code, or secret handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain only natural language and the word basic. There is no code or configuration related to cryptography at those locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Git CLI Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill provides command examples that invoke the external git binary. The commands are read-only, but they still access local repository data and should be run only in repositories the user intends to inspect.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command references are explicit and numerous. The absence of write commands, network transfer commands, and shell pipelines lowers the severity.

content.value_statement: Developers often need quick repository context before reviewing or changing code. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a focused read-only Git command reference.
actual_capabilities: Checks repository status and current branch information.
Lists configured remotes and available branches.
Shows unstaged, staged, and branch comparison diffs.
Reviews commit history, commit details, and file blame output.
Lists tracked files and shows files or commits from Git history.
limitations: It is designed for read-only inspection, not commits, rebases, merges, or pushes.
It does not sanitize sensitive data that may appear in diffs or logs.
It depends on a local Git repository and an available git command.
It does not include GitHub or GitLab API workflows.

use_cases.title: Review Local Changes
use_cases.description: Inspect staged and unstaged changes before asking an AI assistant for a review.

use_cases.title: Understand Branch Context
use_cases.description: Check the active branch, branch list, and commits that differ from the main branch.

use_cases.title: Audit File History
use_cases.description: Use log, blame, and show commands to understand when and why a file changed.

prompt_templates.title: Check Repository Status
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the git skill to check the current repository status and active branch. Summarize only the important state.

prompt_templates.title: Review Current Changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the git skill to inspect unstaged and staged changes. Summarize the changed files and the main behavior changes.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Branch Work
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the git skill to compare this branch with main. Include commit summary, changed files, and notable risk areas.

prompt_templates.title: Investigate File History
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the git skill to inspect the history and blame for the file I name. Explain the most relevant commits and ownership clues.

output_examples.input: Check the repo status and tell me what changed.
output_examples.output: The repository has unstaged changes in two files. One file changes validation behavior, and one updates related documentation.

output: This branch has three commits ahead of main.
Most changes are in the authentication flow.
The largest review area is error handling around expired sessions.

output_examples.input: Show me why this file changed recently.
output_examples.output: Recent history shows a refactor commit followed by a bug fix. The bug fix is likely the relevant change for current behavior.

best_practices: Run commands from the intended repository root.
Review outputs for secrets before sharing them outside the workspace.
Prefer short flags such as --stat, --oneline, and -n for focused results.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for write operations such as commit, merge, rebase, or push.
Do not paste sensitive diffs into external systems without review.
Do not run branch or file placeholders without replacing them with intended values.

faq.question: Does this skill modify a repository?
faq.answer: No. The documented commands are intended for read-only inspection of repository state and history.

faq.question: Can it review staged changes?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes git diff --cached for reviewing staged changes before commit.

faq.question: Can it compare a feature branch with main?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes branch comparison commands for diffs and commit summaries.

faq.question: Does it use GitHub or GitLab APIs?
faq.answer: No. It only documents local Git CLI inspection commands.

faq.question: What data can appear in command output?
faq.answer: Outputs may include file contents, commit messages, author details, branch names, and remote URLs.

faq.question: Is it suitable for private repositories?
faq.answer: Yes, if the user understands that command output may include private repository information.
